semantics: Response and Request retain an input ReadableStream as a managed body edge instead of coercing it to text or consuming it eagerly.
Buffered bodies lazily project one engine-native ReadableStream and keep that identity stable for subsequent body access and consumption.
A null body projects null, remains reusable, and resolves each Body conversion with the appropriate empty representation without becoming disturbed.
bodyUsed is derived from explicit transfer state or the underlying stream disturbed state and therefore changes as soon as consumption begins.
Locked or already-disturbed non-null bodies reject conversion and cloning without falsely changing bodyUsed.
text, JSON, ArrayBuffer, bytes, Blob, and FormData methods share the same reader, queue, cancellation, error, UTF-8, and multipart machinery as the private stream consumers.
Blob conversion carries the normalized Content-Type header while FormData dispatches between URL-encoded and multipart parsing using that header.
Response.clone and Request.clone tee a live stream once, preserve metadata and headers, and give each clone an independently consumable branch.
ReadableStream.tee duplicates ordered chunks without coercion, propagates source errors to both branches, and combines two cancellation reasons before cancelling the source.
Constructing a Request from another Request transfers the body, marks the source used, and rejects consumed or locked source bodies.
All response, request, tee-state, reader, branch, callback, and queued-chunk links are managed Value edges that survive collection and relocation.
Focused Debug, ReleaseSafe, and TSan evidence exercises asynchronous streams, repeated reads, locking, cloning, cancellation, content types, null bodies, and Request transfer.
